jokes.text: Hey! One-percent d-bag! Can't park here. You don't own the sidewalk.
jokes.setup: Liz confronts someone parking improperly
jokes.punchline: Calling them a 'one-percent d-bag' with righteous indignation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon character moment - her social awkwardness manifesting as class warfare rhetoric over parking

jokes.text: despite our tiny, un-American sodas
jokes.setup: Jack praising New York City
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of NYC's soda size regulations as un-American
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack Donaghy - turning a compliment into a political dig with perfect economy and character voice

jokes.text: That's idiotic. So, how was your hiatus? Start with what puzzles you did.
jokes.setup: Someone says NYC is a character on their show
jokes.punchline: Jack's immediate dismissal followed by assumption Liz does puzzles during hiatus
jokes.explanation: Jack's blunt dismissal plus his specific assumption about Liz's leisure activities shows his condescending familiarity

jokes.text: Venice. Solar system. Unicorn. The cast of 'L.A. Law'. That one took me awhile. So much white.
jokes.setup: Liz listing puzzles she completed
jokes.punchline: The L.A. Law cast puzzle was hard because 'so much white'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from innocent puzzle talk to sharp racial observation. Perfect 30 Rock absurdist logic with social commentary

jokes.text: Oh, really, Lemon? You can't even say 'trying'? What positions are you using? The one. There's only one.
jokes.setup: Liz awkwardly says she and Criss are 'trying' for a baby
jokes.punchline: Jack's invasive questioning met with Liz's mortified admission there's only one position
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack-Liz dynamic - his inappropriate boundary-crossing met with her TMI response

jokes.text: thanks to a round of golf with Archbishop Dolan, I was never married
jokes.setup: Jack saying his divorce went smoothly
jokes.punchline: He got the Catholic Church to retroactively annul his existence as married through golf networking
jokes.explanation: Peak 30 Rock absurdism - Jack's corporate networking extending to religious annulment. Perfectly captures his character and the show's satirical worldview

jokes.text: Liddy won a bronze in Horsey-Jumpy at the Baby Olympics
jokes.setup: Jack continuing to brag about his life
jokes.punchline: His baby won a medal in 'Horsey-Jumpy' at Baby Olympics
jokes.explanation: Absurd child-speak name 'Horsey-Jumpy' plus the concept of competitive baby athletics fits Jack's hyper-achieving mindset

jokes.text: If you're tired of sexy vampires, then you'll love 'Hunchbacks', starring Jonathan Silverman as Dr. Fantastico
jokes.setup: Jack pitching a show as alternative to vampire shows
jokes.punchline: Hunchbacks starring B-list actor Jonathan Silverman as 'Dr. Fantastico'
jokes.explanation: Good bad-TV satire with specific casting choice making it funnier. Dr. Fantastico as a character name adds absurdity

jokes.text: do you like the information channel you get when you stay in a hotel? Well, Thursdays is just that now
jokes.setup: Jack asking if she likes hotel info channels
jokes.punchline: That's what NBC Thursdays has become
jokes.explanation: Sharp metaphor for boring television with perfect specificity - hotel info channels are universally recognized as dull

jokes.text: Yes, little big man discovered the grass isn't always greener on the other side and came crawling back
jokes.setup: Jonathan returns to work
jokes.punchline: Jack's condescending 'little big man' and dramatic metaphor for a simple job return
jokes.explanation: Jack's typical condescension toward Jonathan, treating mundane employment like epic narrative

jokes.text: My grandmother was seriously ill. I went to Salinas to feed and bathe her. - I'm sorry. - I still don't like you.
jokes.setup: Jonathan explains his absence with touching family obligation
jokes.punchline: Jack's immediate return to petty dislike despite the sympathy moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - briefly showing humanity before immediately reverting to pettiness. Great character consistency

jokes.text: 'God Cop.' Crime just got a new worst friend.
jokes.setup: Jack reading a show title/tagline
jokes.punchline: 'Worst friend' instead of 'worst enemy' for God as police partner
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of the typical 'worst enemy' tagline structure, plus absurd premise of God as cop partner

jokes.text: It's all explained in the end by the wise black man played by Karl Malone
jokes.setup: Explaining the God Cop show concept
jokes.punchline: The 'wise black man' trope filled by basketball player Karl Malone
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on TV tropes combined with absurd celebrity casting. References Hollywood's problematic racial archetypes

jokes.text: But it's not a yacht for corporate parties and hooker disposal. It's a two-man fishing boat.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his boss bought a boat
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning yacht uses include 'hooker disposal' before the innocent fishing boat reveal
jokes.explanation: Dark corporate satire delivered as casual aside. The contrast between sinister yacht activities and innocent fishing makes both funnier

jokes.text: I believe that is called a skiff. I will not have you telling me boat names in my own office!
jokes.setup: Liz correcting Jack about boat terminology
jokes.punchline: Jack's explosive reaction to being corrected about boats in his office
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack ego moment - his authority threatened by basic nautical vocabulary. The specificity of 'boat names' makes it funnier

jokes.text: 'Oh, Brother'. A comedy about two jive-talking con men hiding out in a monastery.
jokes.setup: Jack pitching show concepts
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous premise combining jive-talking con men with monastery setting
jokes.explanation: Standard bad-TV satire. The incongruous elements (jive talk + monastery) create decent absurdity but feels somewhat generic

jokes.text: The doves I ordered for the wedding came, and they're all dead! One of them touched my tongue!
jokes.setup: Jenna panicking about dead doves for her wedding
jokes.punchline: The specific horror detail that one touched her tongue
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from wedding disaster to personal gross-out. The tongue detail adds visceral comedy to Jenna's drama

jokes.text: Oh, I'm fine, Liz. The doves were just a drill. Everyone needs to be on their toes for my wedding year.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to comfort Jenna about the dead doves
jokes.punchline: Jenna reveals it was a fake emergency drill for her 'wedding year'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism - staging fake emergencies for wedding preparation. The 'wedding year' concept shows her total self-absorption

jokes.text: Wait, if that was a drill, did you kill those doves? No, I bought them at the dead-dove store. Grow up, Liz.
jokes.setup: Liz questioning the logistics of the dove drill
jokes.punchline: Jenna casually mentioning a 'dead-dove store' and telling Liz to grow up
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - 'dead-dove store' as if it's perfectly normal, plus Jenna's condescension for not knowing about it. Peak 30 Rock weirdness

jokes.text: I prefer soul-sucking monster.
jokes.setup: Liz calling Jenna a high-strung perfectionist
jokes.punchline: Jenna's preference for the more extreme descriptor
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna self-awareness about her terrible personality, delivered with pride rather than shame

jokes.text: Beyoncé, J. Lo, Natalie Portman, whose real last name is Hershlag, by the way. Okay, that's not relevant. I just think people should know.
jokes.setup: Jenna listing celebrities who had surprise weddings
jokes.punchline: Random factoid about Natalie Portman's real name with immediate acknowledgment of irrelevance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's scattered celebrity gossip brain and her compulsion to share random trivia

jokes.text: Paz de la Huerta, the former Mrs. Jon Cryer, Honey Boo Boo's mom.
jokes.setup: Jenna listing potential maids of honor
jokes.punchline: Increasingly questionable celebrity connections ending with Honey Boo Boo's mom
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation showing Jenna's diminishing celebrity connections, ending with reality TV bottom-feeding

jokes.text: But you're my oldest friend. And, also, I've known you for a long time.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining why she wants Liz as maid of honor
jokes.punchline: Saying the same thing twice in slightly different words without realizing it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's airheaded redundancy, thinking she's being comprehensive

jokes.text: Oh, God, my hands! Please! I'll do it! I'd love to do it! You're my best friend!
jokes.setup: Liz trying to decline being maid of honor
jokes.punchline: Jenna apparently physically threatening Liz's hands to force agreement
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment showing Jenna's manipulation tactics and Liz's immediate capitulation under pressure

jokes.text: Make sure you have a stripper there from every race. Stan looking for Indian now. It's a tough booking.
jokes.setup: Jenna giving instructions for bachelorette party
jokes.punchline: Casual racism in stripper booking logistics, delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable comedy showing Jenna's oblivious casual racism through entertainment industry logistics

jokes.text: You mean a threesome with Robinson Cano? No! I mean sharing a yogurt
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying he and Hazel reached second base
jokes.punchline: Tracy's baseball player interpretation vs Kenneth's innocent yogurt sharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Tracy's celebrity/sexual worldview vs Kenneth's innocent interpretation. Great misdirection structure

jokes.text: Hazel says she's saving herself for a Grade-A pork machine. And those are expensive.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining Hazel's saving herself
jokes.punchline: She's saving for a meat-grinding machine, not marriage, and Kenneth's concern about cost
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - subverting expectation of sexual saving with literal machinery. Kenneth's practical concern about appliance costs

jokes.text: You're in the kitchen naked, cooking chili. You feed it to her out of your hands. Make her act like a bird.
jokes.setup: Tracy giving relationship advice to Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre romantic scenario ending with bird feeding behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd romantic advice. The bird feeding element adds surreal Tracy logic

jokes.text: That's half as long as it felt like 'Arliss' was on TV.
jokes.setup: Tracy saying he's been married 22 years
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to how long the HBO show Arliss felt like it lasted
jokes.explanation: Sharp TV industry inside joke about a notoriously long-running, little-watched HBO series

jokes.text: If she wants to have a dinner party, you go fry up some dolphin.
jokes.setup: Tracy giving domestic advice about supporting your woman
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting cooking dolphin for dinner party
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy from Tracy's celebrity excess mindset - treating endangered species as dinner party option

jokes.text: Speaking of which, Angie's been in the hospital for a week. Could you go find out why?
jokes.setup: Tracy talking about supporting women
jokes.punchline: Casually asking Kenneth to check on his hospitalized wife as an afterthought
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy self-centeredness - giving marriage advice while neglecting his own wife's hospitalization

jokes.text: Can I have a hot wife? If you gain 50 pounds. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Frank asking about having a hot wife in his sitcom
jokes.punchline: Jack's TV trope requirement that fat guys get hot wives
jokes.explanation: Sharp TV trope observation delivered with perfect economy. Frank's immediate acceptance shows how normalized this is

jokes.text: It's about a boy who befriends a talking panda, and they're allowed to sleep in the same bed.
jokes.setup: Frank pitching a show idea
jokes.punchline: Innocent premise turned creepy with the bed-sharing detail
jokes.explanation: Dark turn on innocent children's show concept. The creepy implication shows Frank's inappropriate mind

jokes.text: I wish. Then I could just solve it with the Schwartzfeld Tesseract.
jokes.setup: Liz saying Jack sees NBC as a business matrix
jokes.punchline: Jack's reference to made-up mathematical solution method
jokes.explanation: Good Jack character moment - his corporate jargon extending to made-up mathematical concepts

jokes.text: Xerox, Alcoa, PAAS. The Easter egg company? They own their market. When was the last time you bought a non-PAAS egg-dyeing kit?
jokes.setup: Jack wishing other companies had bought NBC
jokes.punchline: His passionate defense of PAAS Easter egg dye market dominance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from major corporations to obscure Easter product company. Jack's detailed knowledge of egg-dyeing market share

jokes.text: Their wire egg-dipper is tops in the industry with the thinnest egg-loop to reduce dye lines.
jokes.setup: Jack defending PAAS market dominance
jokes.punchline: Detailed technical knowledge of egg-dyeing equipment specifications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - applying his corporate expertise to ridiculous niche products with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Like when two people run into each other. Sorry, it's the other one. Your next word is 'stare.' Okay, the things you climb to get -- No, it's the other one. It's always the other one!
jokes.setup: Game show contestants trying to guess homonyms
jokes.punchline: Pattern of always guessing wrong homonym with frustrated realization
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the fundamental flaw in the game show format - there's always another homonym

jokes.text: So, you don't peacock them. I don't... think so.
jokes.setup: Jack asking if Liz likes his shows
jokes.punchline: Using 'peacock' as a verb (NBC's logo) with Liz's confused response
jokes.explanation: Decent corporate jargon joke using NBC's peacock logo as verb. Liz's confusion shows how forced it is

jokes.text: Yes, and I thought we'd have a little féte chez nous ce soir.
jokes.setup: Hazel responding to Tracy about dinner party
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top French for simple dinner invitation
jokes.explanation: Standard pretentious character bit. Shows Hazel's trying-too-hard personality but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Oh, how nice to meet a woman who speaks the language of the Nazis' most enthusiastic collaborators.
jokes.setup: Hazel speaking French pretentiously
jokes.punchline: Tracy's historically inaccurate but cutting response about French collaboration
jokes.explanation: Sharp historical reference delivered with Tracy's blunt hostility. Dark but effective put-down of pretentious French

jokes.text: I do enjoy seeing the homes of poor whites.
jokes.setup: Tracy being invited to Kenneth and Hazel's home
jokes.punchline: Classist anthropological attitude toward 'poor whites'
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy character moment showing his celebrity condescension expressed through racial/class dynamics

jokes.text: Yes, I'm sort of the black Tyler Perry.
jokes.setup: Hazel describing her movie studio
jokes.punchline: Calling herself 'the black Tyler Perry' despite being white
jokes.explanation: Absurd self-description showing Hazel's delusion and cultural insensitivity. Good character-revealing moment

jokes.text: whose most recent credits include running onstage during 'Sister Act'
jokes.setup: Hazel describing herself as an aspiring actress
jokes.punchline: Her 'credit' is disrupting a Broadway show
jokes.explanation: Good cringe character moment showing Hazel's delusional view of her disruptive behavior as legitimate acting credit

jokes.text: You know, I wasn't scripted to be in that episode. I just wandered onto set because Reginald Vel Johnson owed me $40.
jokes.setup: Reference to Tracy being on Family Matters
jokes.punchline: He wasn't actually cast, just collecting debt from Carl Winslow actor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy backstory explaining random TV appearance through mundane debt collection. Perfect character logic

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I'm the most stable adult here.
jokes.setup: Tracy realizing everyone's asking him for advice
jokes.punchline: The realization that he's somehow the most stable person
jokes.explanation: Excellent character irony - Tracy Jordan as voice of reason is perfectly absurd but somehow true

jokes.text: She wants her something borrowed to be Jessica Biel's youth.
jokes.setup: Liz complaining about being Jenna's maid of honor
jokes.punchline: Jenna wants to borrow youth from a celebrity for wedding tradition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna absurdity - treating abstract concepts like youth as borrowable wedding items. Great celebrity specificity

jokes.text: Thank you for saying that in dated urban slang so that I'll understand you.
jokes.setup: Tracy suggesting Liz should 'tank it'
jokes.punchline: Liz's sarcastic acknowledgment of Tracy's attempt at relatability
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about Tracy's condescending attempt to connect with Liz through outdated slang

jokes.text: Because you're terrified of her, the way most white ladies are with their best friends? That is racist and only pan true.
jokes.setup: Tracy asking why Liz won't disappoint Jenna
jokes.punchline: Liz's malapropism 'pan true' instead of 'partially true' while denying racism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Liz's nervous malapropism undermining her denial of Tracy's accurate observation

jokes.text: Tonight on NBC, Joe Rogan is 'Mandela'. And, if it's Wednesday, it must be 'Cricket Night in America'. Then, on 'Jay', a full hour of Gary Sinise's band.
jokes.setup: Jack announcing NBC programming
jokes.punchline: Increasingly terrible show concepts culminating in Gary Sinise's band
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of terrible TV concepts. Gary Sinise's band as a full hour of programming is beautifully specific and absurd

jokes.text: Hang on. Did you cast yourself in this show? What can I say? We saw hundreds of actors for God. Finally the network executive said I should just play him. You're the network executive.
jokes.setup: Liz questioning Jack's God Cop casting
jokes.punchline: Jack's circular justification where he cast himself based on his own recommendation
jokes.explanation: Good circular logic joke showing Jack's self-serving decision-making disguised as professional process

jokes.text: I have an investor lined up who will buy the network and keep me in charge. I can't tell you who it is, but his word is as good as the color consistency on one of his Easter eggs.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his secret investor
jokes.punchline: Obvious reference to PAAS Easter egg company through quality metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the PAAS obsession with Jack's attempt at subtle hint through quality metaphor

jokes.text: Adolf Paas?
jokes.setup: Liz guessing the Easter egg company investor
jokes.punchline: Adding 'Adolf' to make PAAS sound like a Nazi name
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay connecting PAAS to Adolf Hitler, though somewhat lazy Nazi reference

jokes.text: I cannot go to another business-school reunion and sit at the non-CEO table with the... women and nice men.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his motivation for the NBC scheme
jokes.punchline: His casual sexism and dismissal of 'nice men' as failures
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment showing his corporate masculinity anxiety and casual discrimination

jokes.text: Before this, I ran an all-women's theater company. Everyone was vice president.
jokes.setup: Liz saying she's never worked at a properly managed company
jokes.punchline: Her previous company's egalitarian title structure
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Liz's liberal/feminist background through workplace structure comedy

jokes.text: Look at Sherman's march to the sea. Or what my good friend Bane tried to do to Gotham.
jokes.setup: Jack giving historical examples of destruction for renewal
jokes.punchline: Comparing Civil War history to Batman villain, calling Bane his 'good friend'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from real history to fictional supervillainy. Jack's casual friendship with comic book terrorist perfect character beat

jokes.text: Who do you think you are? God? We're ready for you.
jokes.setup: Liz asking Jack who he thinks he is
jokes.punchline: Perfect timing as crew calls for Jack (playing God) to set
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing with the crew call interrupting at exactly the right moment for maximum irony

jokes.text: If there is one thing I have realized from being God, it's that the more you know, the more you realize you do not... know.
jokes.setup: Jack delivering lines as God in his show
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-philosophical wisdom delivered with dramatic pause
jokes.explanation: Generic pseudo-profound dialogue typical of bad TV. Shows Jack's pretentious writing and self-importance

jokes.text: Why is he learning anything? He's supposed to be God.
jokes.setup: Watching Jack's God character deliver learning-based wisdom
jokes.punchline: Logical objection to omniscient deity having revelations
jokes.explanation: Good logical comedy pointing out the theological flaw in Jack's God character having character development

jokes.text: Well, it's not technically a home, because no one's supposed to be living here, but welcome to our condemned site of the Candyman murders.
jokes.setup: Hazel welcoming Tracy to their home
jokes.punchline: Reveal that it's an illegal residence at a murder site
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from housing confession to murder site revelation. Shows Hazel's poor life choices

jokes.text: If you put this in the oven at about 200 degrees, my snake should be very comfortable while she gives birth.
jokes.setup: Hazel giving cooking instructions
jokes.punchline: It's actually for her pregnant snake, not food
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from cooking to reptile birth assistance. Shows Hazel's unconventional lifestyle priorities

jokes.text: Thank you. Your home is terrible.
jokes.setup: Tracy being offered appetizers in the terrible living situation
jokes.punchline: His blunt honesty about their home while accepting hospitality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy character moment - his brutal honesty delivered with complete sincerity and no self-awareness

jokes.text: Oh, I always have one white person in all my movies. We have to have a villain. Oh, and who's worse than us whites, right? I mean, look at slavery. That was bananas!
jokes.setup: Tracy saying he always casts white villains
jokes.punchline: Hazel's enthusiastic agreement escalating to inappropriate slavery comment
jokes.explanation: Cringe escalation showing Hazel's desperate pandering through inappropriate historical commentary

jokes.text: That sturgeon just will not die. Excuse me a moment. Oh, I also do accents. Oh, me a Cockney person.
jokes.setup: Hazel trying to impress Tracy with her acting skills
jokes.punchline: Interrupted by fish-killing duty, terrible accent demonstration
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hazel's poor priorities (impressing guest vs killing fish) and terrible acting ability

jokes.text: Zoltan is an 18-year-old German shepherd who cannot walk. So, you're gonna be in charge of him. No, Jenna, I'm allergic to dogs. Oh, don't worry. He's hairless because of his insanity medication.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining ring bearer duties with elderly dog
jokes.punchline: Dog can't walk, is hairless from mental health medication
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of wedding nightmare details. 'Insanity medication' for dogs is perfect absurd specificity

jokes.text: The cake is a tasteless foam. There's no cake?
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining molecular gastronomy wedding cake
jokes.punchline: Liz's horror at literal interpretation of 'tasteless foam'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on 'tasteless' with Liz's practical concern about actual cake absence

jokes.text: 'Tank It,' the reality event of the year, where we make grandpas put on tank tops and then laugh at them.
jokes.setup: Jack announcing another NBC show
jokes.punchline: Show concept based on tank/tank top pun, elderly humiliation
jokes.explanation: Decent pun-based show concept with uncomfortable elder abuse undertones typical of reality TV

jokes.text: And there's nothing worse than a surprise Lemon party.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining surprise bachelorette party
jokes.punchline: Unintentional sexual reference through her last name
jokes.explanation: Double entendre playing on Liz's name and internet slang. Works for character obliviousness

jokes.text: My foot? No, that's shoe stuffing. It helps keep the shape of my shoes. Why was you rubbing my foot, anyway?
jokes.setup: Hazel claiming she was rubbing Tracy's foot
jokes.punchline: Tracy's confusion about shoe maintenance being mistaken for foot
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection about shoe care vs seduction. Tracy's practical confusion about the situation

jokes.text: Ooh, I smell Italian guys.
jokes.setup: Jenna arriving at her bachelorette party
jokes.punchline: Her ethnic fetishization of male strippers
jokes.explanation: Standard Jenna inappropriate comment showing her objectifying mindset at bachelorette party

jokes.text: Bethany from work is here and a bunch of my neighbors. A clown. And there's a 'God Cop' marathon on NBC.
jokes.setup: Liz revealing the actual bachelorette party
jokes.punchline: Increasingly sad party elements ending with Jack's terrible show
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Liz's sad party planning culminating in callback to Jack's awful programming

jokes.text: Listen up, ladies. You're all in a lot of trouble if you don't protect yourselves against identity theft. Please take a pamphlet. Pass them along.
jokes.setup: Police officer arriving at bachelorette party
jokes.punchline: Instead of stripping, he's doing identity theft education
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection subverting stripper cop expectation with mundane public service announcement

jokes.text: So am I. And if he ever wakes up from that coma, I'm dead! That's why I live for today.
jokes.setup: Hazel responding to Tracy saying he's married
jokes.punchline: Her husband is in a coma and she's apparently done something terrible
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy revealing Hazel's criminal past and using philosophical cliché to justify bad behavior

jokes.text: Because you're not famous! You think I want someone up there with me that other people are looking at? I wanted you because you're nothing!
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining why she chose Liz as maid of honor
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about wanting Liz specifically because she's unremarkable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism moment with devastating honesty about her friendship motivations. Excellent character revelation

jokes.text: I get it. You didn't want to get Pippa Middleton-ed. Why would you even mention her?!
jokes.setup: Liz understanding Jenna's fear of being upstaged
jokes.punchline: Jenna's horror at mention of famous maid of honor who upstaged bride
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity reference joke about maid of honor upstaging with Jenna's visceral reaction to the concept

jokes.text: And what if she came in in a wheelchair and then stood up and everyone applauded and cried?
jokes.setup: Jenna getting excited about being her own maid of honor
jokes.punchline: Fake disability reveal for wedding drama attention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment showing her willingness to fake disability for attention. Cringe but character-accurate

jokes.text: You just called me 'Liz'! We can fix that in the cutting room.
jokes.setup: Liz noticing Jack called her by her first name instead of 'Lemon'
jokes.punchline: Jack's response treating real life like a TV production
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke with Jack's TV industry mindset bleeding into real conversation. Character-appropriate confusion

jokes.text: Not! What I said was I would never sleep with Tracy for a pan in a movie.
jokes.setup: Hazel denying she came on to Tracy
jokes.punchline: Using 'not' joke + saying 'pan' instead of 'part' in movie
jokes.explanation: Standard malapropism humor with dated 'not' joke structure. Shows character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Come on, Kenmore washers and dryers, who are you gonna believe here?
jokes.setup: Tracy asking Kenneth to choose who to believe
jokes.punchline: Addressing Kenneth by appliance brand instead of name
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tracy absurdism using appliance brand as nickname. Shows his scattered celebrity brain

jokes.text: It's a blessing and a purse.
jokes.setup: Tracy saying he's never wrong
jokes.punchline: Saying 'purse' instead of 'curse' for being always right
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy malapropism turning 'curse' into feminine accessory. Character-appropriate word confusion

jokes.text: And both sides being right is like kissing your sister. A wonderful treat!
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to resolve Tracy-Hazel conflict logically
jokes.punchline: Innocent enthusiasm about incestuous comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence about inappropriate sayings. Dark humor from his enthusiastic misunderstanding

jokes.text: I forgot my sports bra, so, ironically --
jokes.setup: Liz explaining she actually hurt herself at breast health 5K
jokes.punchline: The irony of getting breast injury at breast health event
jokes.explanation: Decent ironic humor about injury at health event. Shows Liz's physical incompetence

jokes.text: No! You forgot it could also be an exclamation about a fruit, as in, 'Oh, pear!'
jokes.setup: Contestant correctly identifying 'au pair' on homonym show
jokes.punchline: Host revealing ridiculous third interpretation as fruit exclamation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist game show logic creating impossible homonym through forced fruit exclamation
